text: I switched from a $100-tier competitor and lost nothing. At $29, this almost feels like charity.
Exports are reliable. Mobile occasionally needs a second refresh.
$29 is already the top of my budget. If it goes up, I will have to return to spreadsheets.
Support replies incredibly fast. That is impressive for a solo company.
Please add dark mode.
I tried the $59 and $45 competitors. Somehow this $29 product is still the smoothest.
Every dollar matters to an independent developer. Raise the price and I am gone.
Clear API documentation. I integrated it in half a day.
I am extremely budget-sensitive. The $29 price is why I signed up.
The automatic weekly reports saved my life.
It can load slowly sometimes, but overall I am satisfied.
I am a student and $29 is already a stretch. If it rises, I will cancel.
The template library keeps getting better.
Honestly, with this feature set at this price, does the founder know how to price a product?
I saw the pricing rumor on the forum. At $49 I will cancel immediately. That is my budget.
I would love support for switching between multiple workspaces.
The dashboard is better than the last two tools I used combined.
Onboarding is a little confusing, but it works well once you learn it.
I use it for a side project and $29/month already hurts. A price increase means cutting this subscription.
Updates ship frequently. The developer clearly works hard.
My colleague pays $59 for a competitor. When they saw this had more features for $29, they went quiet.
A few more integrations would make this perfect.
Reliability has improved noticeably over the last six months.
Freelance income is unpredictable. Affordable tools like this are essential. Please do not raise the price.
The custom reports are surprisingly powerful.
Please offer a discounted annual plan.
This is the best-value product in my entire subscription list. Nothing else comes close.
If prices rise, at least keep the old price for existing users. The original landing page promised that.
The mobile experience is better than I expected.
I have used it for eight months and now depend on it.
